label: Model Selection for Offline Reinforcement Learning: Practical Considerations for
authors: Shengpu Tang, Jenna Wiens
detail: Investigates model selection for offline RL in healthcare using OPE as a proxy for validation. Finds FQE consistently leads to best validation ranking. Proposes a two-stage approach to accelerate model selection. (Found in missions: 05)
scan_relevance: CORE

label: Offline Reinforcement Learning with Uncertainty for Treatment Strategies in Seps
authors: Ran Liu, Joseph L. Greenstein, James C. Fackler, Jules Bergmann, Melania M. Bembea, Raimond L. Winslow
detail: Novel application of RL for sepsis treatment recommendations with confidence estimation. Identifies RL bias against aggressive intervention due to confounding. Mitigates bias using subspace learning. (Found in missions: 05)
scan_relevance: CORE

label: Proximal Reinforcement Learning: Efficient Off-Policy Evaluation in Partially Ob
authors: Andrew Bennett, Nathan Kallus
detail: Extends proximal causal inference to POMDPs for off-policy evaluation. Introduces bridge functions for identification. Demonstrates on sepsis management. (Found in missions: 05)
scan_relevance: SUPPORTING

label: Multi-Objective SPIBB: Seldonian Offline Policy Improvement with Safety Constrai
authors: Harsh Satija, Philip S. Thomas, Joelle Pineau, Romain Laroche
detail: Safe Policy Improvement under constraints with multiple rewards. Provides high-probability guarantees. Evaluated on IV fluids and vasopressors for sepsis treatment. (Found in missions: 05)
scan_relevance: CORE

label: Semi-Markov Offline Reinforcement Learning for Healthcare
authors: Mehdi Fatemi, Mary Wu, Jeremy Petch, Walter Nelson, Stuart J. Connolly, Alexander Benz, Anthony Carnicelli, Marzyeh Ghassemi
detail: Proposes Semi-MDP framework for offline RL with variable-time actions. Introduces SDQN, SDDQN, SBCQ algorithms. Demonstrates MDP methods fail on variable-time environments. Validated on warfarin dosing. (Found in missions: 05)
scan_relevance: CORE

label: Towards Safe Mechanical Ventilation Treatment Using Deep Offline Reinforcement L
authors: Flemming Kondrup, Thomas Jiralerspong, Elaine Lau, Nathan de Lara, Jacob Shkrob, My Duc Tran, Doina Precup, Sumana Basu
detail: DeepVent: CQL-based agent for ventilator parameter optimization. Clinically relevant intermediate reward addresses sparse rewards. Recommends parameters within safe ranges per clinical trials. (Found in missions: 05)
scan_relevance: CORE

label: Offline Reinforcement Learning for Safer Blood Glucose Control in People with Ty
authors: Harry Emerson, Matthew Guy, Ryan McConville
detail: Evaluates BCQ, CQL and TD3-BC for insulin dosing. Trained on less than a tenth of online RL samples. Increases time in healthy range from 61.6% to 65.3% without increasing hypoglycemia. (Found in missions: 05)
scan_relevance: CORE

label: Deep Offline Reinforcement Learning for Real-world Treatment Optimization Applic
authors: Milashini Nambiar, Supriyo Ghosh, Priscilla Ong, Yu En Chan, Yong Mong Bee, Pavitra Krishnaswamy
detail: Introduces transition sampling to address action imbalance in offline RL. Compares DDQN vs CQL on diabetes and sepsis tasks. Shows substantial improvements over standard CQL. (Found in missions: 05)
scan_relevance: CORE

label: Delphic Offline Reinforcement Learning under Nonidentifiable Hidden Confounding
authors: Alizée Pace, Hugo Yèche, Bernhard Schölkopf, Gunnar Rätsch, Guy Tennenholtz
detail: Tackles hidden confounding in nonidentifiable offline RL settings. Proposes delphic uncertainty from world model variation. Constructs pessimistic offline RL algorithm. Validated on sepsis benchmark and EHR. (Found in missions: 05)
scan_relevance: CORE

label: Towards Real-World Applications of Personalized Anesthesia Using Policy Constrai
authors: Xiuding Cai, Jiao Chen, Yaoyao Zhu, Beimin Wang, Yu Yao
detail: Proposes Policy Constraint Q-Learning (PCQL) for anesthesia. Adds policy constraint to CQL to keep agent distribution close to anesthesiologist. SHAP interpretability analysis. (Found in missions: 05)
scan_relevance: SUPPORTING

label: Reinforcement Learning in Dynamic Treatment Regimes Needs Critical Reexamination
authors: Zhiyao Luo, Yangchen Pan, Peter Watkinson, Tingting Zhu
detail: Critical examination of offline RL in DTRs. With 17,000+ experiments on sepsis data, shows RL performance varies significantly with evaluation metrics and MDP formulations. RL can be surpassed by random baselines. (Found in missions: 05)
scan_relevance: CORE

label: Empowering Clinicians with Medical Decision Transformers: A Framework for Sepsis
authors: Aamer Abdul Rahman, Pranav Agarwal, Rita Noumeir, Philippe Jouvet, Vincent Michalski, Samira Ebrahimi Kahou
detail: Medical Decision Transformer (MeDT) based on goal-conditioned RL. Uses acuity scores for conditioning. Enables clinician-model interaction. Competitive with CQL, BCQ while more interpretable. (Found in missions: 05)
scan_relevance: CORE

label: Offline Guarded Safe Reinforcement Learning for Medical Treatment Optimization S
authors: Runze Yan, Xun Shen, Akifumi Wachi, Sebastien Gros, Anni Zhao, Xiao Hu
detail: Proposes OGSRL with dual constraint mechanism: OOD guardian for clinically validated regions and safety cost constraint encoding physiological boundaries. Theoretical guarantees on safety and near-optimality. (Found in missions: 05)
scan_relevance: CORE

label: Hybrid Adaptive Conformal Offline Reinforcement Learning for Fair Population Hea
authors: Sanjay Basu, Sadiq Y. Patel, Parth Sheth, Bhairavi Muralidharan, Namrata Elamaran, Aakriti Kinra, Rajaie Batniji
detail: HACO framework: separates risk calibration from preference optimization. Uses conformal threshold for safe action masking. Validated on 2.77M decisions across 168K patients. AUC ~0.81 for risk discrimination. (Found in missions: 05)
scan_relevance: CORE

label: From Robotics to Sepsis Treatment: Offline RL via Geometric Pessimism
authors: Sarthak Wanjari
detail: Geo-IQL: augments IQL with kNN-based density penalty for OOD conservatism. On MIMIC-III sepsis, standard IQL collapses to BC (75% agreement) while Geo-IQL achieves 86.4% terminal agreement. (Found in missions: 05)
scan_relevance: CORE

label: Ensuring Safety in Automated Mechanical Ventilation through Offline Reinforcemen
authors: Hang Yu, Huidong Liu, Qingchen Zhang, William Joy, Kateryna Nikulina, Andreas A. Schuppert, Sina Saffaran, Declan Bates
detail: Transformer-based CQL (T-CQL) with uncertainty quantification for mechanical ventilation. Digital twin for online bedside evaluation. Outperforms state-of-the-art offline RL methods. (Found in missions: 05)
scan_relevance: CORE

label: Insulin4RL: Real-Time Insulin Management in the Intensive Care Unit for Offline
authors: Thomas Frost, Steve Harris
detail: Insulin4RL dataset: naturally irregular inputs and actions from MIMIC-IV. 375K+ decisions across 12,209 patients for insulin infusion titration. Baseline metrics using model-free offline RL. (Found in missions: 05)
scan_relevance: CORE

label: Synthetic Acute Hypotension and Sepsis Datasets Based on MIMIC-III and Published
authors: Nicholas I-Hsien Kuo, Mark Polizzotto, Simon Finfer, Louisa Jorm, Sebastiano Barbieri
detail: Synthetic datasets for acute hypotension and sepsis using GANs and MIMIC-III. Very low identity disclosure risk (0.045%). Published as part of the Health Gym project for offline RL development. (Found in missions: 05)
scan_relevance: CORE

label: Offline Policy Evaluation and Optimization under Confounding
authors: Chinmaya Kausik, Yangyi Lu, Kevin Tan, Maggie Makar, Yixin Wang, Ambuj Tewari
detail: Maps landscape of offline policy evaluation under confounding. Characterizes settings where consistent estimates are provably impossible. Provides lower bound estimation algorithms. Evaluated on sepsis management. (Found in missions: 05)
scan_relevance: SUPPORTING

label: Predicting the Need for Blood Transfusion in Intensive Care Units with Reinforce
authors: Yuqing Wang, Yun Zhao, Linda Petzold
detail: Uses discretized BCQ for transfusion decision-making. Evaluates state representation and reward design. Transfer learning improves performance on data-scarce datasets by up to 17%. (Found in missions: 05)
scan_relevance: SUPPORTING

label: Horizon Reduction as Information Loss in Offline Reinforcement Learning
authors: Uday Kumar Nidadala, Venkata Bhumika Guthi
detail: Proves horizon reduction in offline RL can induce fundamental information loss. Identifies three structural failure modes: prefix indistinguishability, objective misspecification, and representation aliasing. Optimal policies may be indistinguishable from suboptimal ones even with infinite data. (Found in missions: 05)
scan_relevance: SUPPORTING

label: Optimizing Warfarin Dosing Using Contextual Bandit: An Offline Policy Learning a
authors: Yong Huang, Charles A. Downs, Amir M. Rahmani
detail: Uses offline policy learning in contextual bandit setting for warfarin dosing. Learned policies surpass baselines without genotype inputs, even with suboptimal demonstrations. (Found in missions: 05)
scan_relevance: SUPPORTING

label: Flexible Blood Glucose Control: Offline Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedba
authors: Harry Emerson, Sam Gordon James, Matthew Guy, Ryan McConville
detail: PAINT framework: offline RL from human feedback for insulin dosing. Uses sketch-based reward learning from patient records. Reduces glycaemic risk by 15% over commercial benchmark. (Found in missions: 05)
scan_relevance: SUPPORTING

label: Guardian-regularized Safe Offline Reinforcement Learning for Smart Weaning of Me
authors: Aysin Tumay, Sophia Sun, Sonia Fereidooni, Aaron Dumas, Elise Jortberg, Rose Yu
detail: Clinically-aware OOD-regularized Model-based Policy Optimization (CORMPO) for weaning MCS devices. Transformer-based digital twin for evaluation. 28% higher reward than baselines and 82.6% higher in clinical metrics. (Found in missions: 05)
scan_relevance: SUPPORTING

label: Toward Foundation Model for Multivariate Wearable Sensing of Physiological Signa
authors: Yunfei Luo, Yuliang Chen, Asif Salekin, Tauhidur Rahman
headline: First multimodal wearable foundation model (NormWear) using channel-aware attention with CLS token, pretrained on PPG/ECG/EEG/GSR/IMU, evaluated across 11 datasets and 18 applications.
scan_relevance: Core reference for NormWear 768-dim embeddings from PPG/accelerometer. Directly relevant to the next-step Sensor Foundation Model goal.

label: Toward World Modeling of Physiological Signals with Chaos-Theoretic Balancing an
authors: Yunfei Luo, Xi Chen, Yuliang Chen, Lanshuang Zhang, Md Mofijul Islam, Siwei Zhao, Peter Kotanko, Subhasis Dasgupta, Andrew Campbell, Rakesh Malhotra, Tauhidur Rahman
headline: NormWear-2 extends the foundation model approach to world modeling; forecasts physiological signals across multiple time scales using chaos-theoretic balancing and latent state transition adaptation.
scan_relevance: Direct successor to NormWear; demonstrates forecasting and intervention-conditioned modeling for physiological time series.

label: Scaling Wearable Foundation Models (LSM)
authors: Girish Narayanswamy, Xin Liu, Kumar Ayush, Yuzhe Yang, Xuhai Xu, Shun Liao, Jake Garrison, Shyam Tailor, Jake Sunshine, Yun Liu, Tim Althoff, Shrikanth Narayanan, Pushmeet Kohli, Jiening Zhan, Mark Malhotra, Shwetak Patel, Samy Abdel-Ghaffar, Daniel McDuff
headline: LSM: a multimodal foundation model trained on 40M hours of wearable sensor data from 165K people. Establishes scaling laws for imputation, interpolation, and extrapolation.
scan_relevance: Key reference for wearable foundation model scaling; includes imputation as a core task; largest wearable-signals dataset to date.

label: Physical activities enable scalable foundation modelling for broad-spectrum heal
authors: Zhenghuang Wu, Yuyao Zhu, Songlin Xu
headline: StepFM: a foundation model built solely on step counter data. Privacy-preserving, computation-efficient. Transferred to >20 health risk prediction tasks.
scan_relevance: Demonstrates low-dimensional step data as viable foundation model input with privacy benefits.

label: Inertia-1: An Open Exploration of Wearable Motion Foundation Models
authors: Zongzhe Xu, Aakarsh Anand, Sarah Jiang, Chuntung Zhuang, Zitao Shuai, Sriram Sankararaman, Yuzhe Yang
headline: Comprehensive exploration using >18.2M hours of accelerometer data. Evaluates data choices, model choices, and training choices across 15 datasets.
scan_relevance: Provides design recipes for motion foundation models; key resource for building accelerometer-based foundation models.

label: SiamQuality: A ConvNet-Based Foundation Model for Imperfect Physiological Signal
authors: Cheng Ding, Zhicheng Guo, Zhaoliang Chen, Randall J Lee, Cynthia Rudin, Xiao Hu
headline: SIAMQUALITY uses Siamese self-supervised learning on 36M PPG pairs for robust representations. CNN backbone outperforms transformers on imperfect physiological data.
scan_relevance: Directly addresses signal quality in foundation model training; robust to noisy and incomplete physiological data.

label: A robust PPG foundation model using multimodal physiological supervision
authors: Eloy Geenjaar, Vince Calhoun, Scott Daly, Gouthaman KV, Lie Lu, Trisha Mittal, Daniel P. Darcy
headline: PPG foundation model leveraging ECG/respiratory signals for contrastive pretraining. Handles noisy PPG without requiring high-quality pretraining data.
scan_relevance: Multimodal supervision for robust PPG representation; improved 14/15 downstream tasks with 3x fewer subjects.

label: Multiple Imputation Approaches for Epoch-level Accelerometer data in Trials
authors: Mia S. Tackney, Elizabeth Williamson, Derek G. Cook, Elizabeth Limb, Tess Harris, James Carpenter
headline: Epoch-level (5-second) missing data framework for accelerometer trials. Parametric and non-parametric MI approaches. Validated on PACE-UP Trial.
scan_relevance: Directly addresses missing accelerometer data in clinical trials with epoch-level granularity.

label: A framework for handling missing accelerometer outcome data in trials
authors: Mia S. Tackney, Derek G. Cook, Daniel Stahl, Khalida Ismail, Elizabeth Williamson, James Carpenter
headline: Analysis framework using wear time to define missingness on the epoch level; wear-time-informed MI for accelerometer outcomes.
scan_relevance: Practical framework for non-wear and missing data handling in accelerometer-based trials.

label: DynImp: Dynamic Imputation for Wearable Sensing Data Through Sensory and Tempora
authors: Zepeng Huo, Taowei Ji, Yifei Liang, Shuai Huang, Zhangyang Wang, Xiaoning Qian, Bobak Mortazavi
headline: DynImp uses nearest-neighbor feature-axis imputation plus LSTM-based denoising autoencoder. Tested at >50% missing rates.
scan_relevance: Handles extreme missingness by leveraging cross-sensor correlations and temporal dynamics.

label: VCR: Learning Valid Contextual Representation for Incomplete Wearable Signals
authors: Yuxuan Weng, Wenhan Luo, Qijia Shao
headline: Self-supervised framework using orthogonal tokenizer and missing-aware Mixture-of-Experts. Reconstructs only shared components to avoid hallucination.
scan_relevance: Novel missing modality handling for wearable data; avoids hallucinating non-inferable modality details.

label: Self-Supervised and Topological Signal-Quality Assessment for Any PPG Device
authors: Wei Shao, Ruoyu Zhang, Zequan Liang, Ehsan Kourkchi, Setareh Rafatirad, Houman Homayoun
headline: First fully unsupervised PPG SQA combining contrastive 1D-ResNet with topological data analysis. Cross-device without re-tuning.
scan_relevance: Unsupervised quality assessment working across heterogeneous PPG devices; no labeled data required.

label: Lightweight ResNet-Based Deep Learning for Photoplethysmography Signal Quality A
authors: Yangyang Zhao, Matti Kaisti, Olli Lahdenoja, Jonas Sandelin, Arman Anzanpour, Joonas Lehto, Joel Nuotio, Jussi Jaakkola, Arto Relander, Tuija Vasankari, Juhani Airaksinen, Tuomas Kiviniemi, Tero Koivisto
headline: Lightweight ResNet with Squeeze-and-Excitation for PPG SQA. >99% parameter reduction; 96.52% AUC on M4M dataset.
scan_relevance: Efficient PPG quality assessment suitable for on-device wearable deployment.

label: Reduction of motion artifacts from photoplethysmography signals using learned co
authors: Giulio Basso, Xi Long, Reinder Haakma, Rik Vullings
headline: Algorithm unfolding combines signal decomposition with deep learning for PPG motion artifact removal. SNR improved from -7.07 to 11.23 dB.
scan_relevance: Interpretable motion artifact removal using learned dictionaries; validated on PPG-DaLiA.

label: SpO2 Predictor-Guided Stage-Wise Time-Frequency Reconstruction of Low-Quality Du
authors: Zequan Liang, Elahe Hosseini, Ning Miao, Mahdi Pirayesh Shirazi Nejad, Wei Shao, Ehsan Kourkchi, Setareh Rafatirad, Houman Homayoun
headline: Stage-wise time-frequency reconstruction for low-quality dual-wavelength PPG, guided by pretrained SpO2 predictor.
scan_relevance: Novel PPG reconstruction preserving physiologically relevant SpO2 information beyond waveform fidelity.

label: Versatile Cardiovascular Signal Generation with a Unified Diffusion Transformer
authors: Zehua Chen, Yuyang Miao, Liyuan Wang, Luyun Fan, Danilo P. Mandic, Jun Zhu
headline: UniCardio: multi-modal diffusion transformer for PPG/ECG/BP signal denoising, imputation, and translation in a unified generative framework.
scan_relevance: Unified approach to cardiovascular signal quality improvement; handles denoising and imputation jointly.

label: DEM: A Distilled Explanation Model for Interpretable Anomaly Detection in Physio
authors: Jyotirmoy Singh, Anushka Roy, Shreea Bose, Chittaranjan Hota
headline: Glass-box anomaly detection distilling XGBoost into interpretable decision trees. Handles false alarms from missing data and sensor faults.
scan_relevance: Handles missing data and sensor faults with interpretable outputs for WBANs.

label: MuteBench: Modality Unavailability Tolerance Evaluation for Incomplete Multimoda
authors: Wugeng Zheng, Ziwen Kan, Tianlong Chen, Chen Chen, Song Wang
headline: Benchmark evaluating 6 fusion architectures under modality missing and within-modality missing across 9 clinical datasets (125K samples).
scan_relevance: Comprehensive benchmark for missing modalities in clinical AI including wearable contexts.

label: Leveraging Imperfect Medical Data: A Manifold-Consistent Spatio-Temporal Network
authors: Jiangtao Fan, Anish Jindal, Amir Atapour-Abarghouei
headline: MCSTN handles missing measurements, sensor failures, and noise via dual-level corruption modeling and manifold consistency. PAMAP2, Opportunity, WISDM.
scan_relevance: Robust HAR under imperfect sensing addressing real-world wearable sensor failures.

label: Decomposing and Fusing Intra- and Inter-Sensor Spatio-Temporal Signal for Multi-
authors: Haoyu Xie, Haoxuan Li, Chunyuan Zheng, Haonan Yuan, Guorui Liao, Jun Liao, Li Liu
headline: DecomposeWHAR: decomposes multi-sensor signals into intra- and inter-sensor components using depthwise separable conv, SSM, and self-attention.
scan_relevance: Novel decomposition-fusion paradigm modeling intra- and inter-sensor relationships separately.

label: A Comparison of Fusion Techniques for Multi-Modal HAR on the HARMES Dataset
authors: Ahmed Mohamady, Robin Burchard, Kristof Van Laerhoven
headline: Systematic comparison of 7 fusion techniques (IMU + audio + humidity). Gated multimodal fusion achieves macro F1=0.82.
scan_relevance: Head-to-head comparison; practical guidance for sensor fusion in multi-modal wearables.

label: FairTune: A Bias-Aware Fine-Tuning Framework Towards Fair Heart Rate Prediction
authors: Lovely Yeswanth Panchumarthi, Saurabh Kataria, Yi Wu, Xiao Hu, Alex Fedorov, Hyunjung Gloria Kwak
headline: Fine-tuning PPG foundation models can widen gender fairness gaps despite reducing MAE up to 80%. GroupDRO and inverse frequency weighting mitigate this.
scan_relevance: Critical finding about demographic bias amplification during wearable sensor FM fine-tuning.

label: Evidence for Phenotype-Driven Disparities in Freezing of Gait Detection and Appr
authors: Timothy Odonga, Christine D. Esper, Stewart A. Factor, J. Lucas McKay, Hyeokhyen Kwon
headline: HAR models for FOG detection exhibit bias across age, sex, disease duration, and FOG phenotype. Transfer learning improves fairness.
scan_relevance: Shows demographic and clinical biases in wearable sensor models; transfer learning as mitigation.

label: A Bayesian Functional Accelerated Failure-Time Model with Varying Effects Correc
authors: Joseph Yang, Roger Zoh, Carmen Tekwe, Lan Xue
headline: Bayesian functional AFT model addressing measurement error in wearable data. Models subgroup effects across race/region for stroke risk.
scan_relevance: Statistical framework for measurement error correction in wearable accelerometer data with demographic heterogeneity.

label: Lightweight Cross-Device Sleep Tracking on the WeBe Wearable Platform
authors: Wei Shao, Ehsan Kourkchi, Krishi Prashant Shah, Zequan Liang, Setareh Rafatirad, Houman Homayoun
headline: Lightweight sleep pipeline from raw accelerometer using globally calibrated threshold. Cross-device validation (WeBe vs ActiGraph). TST error 27.4 min.
scan_relevance: Cross-device calibration for accelerometer sleep tracking; simple, reproducible pipeline.

label: BCoughBench: Benchmarking Respiratory Acoustic Foundation Models Under Body-Coup
authors: Mayur Sanap, Prasanna Desikan, Edgar Lobaton
headline: Benchmarks 5 respiratory FMs under body-coupled wearable conditions. Mean AUROC declines from 0.785 to 0.689-0.723. Sex classification collapses.
scan_relevance: Negative result: FM performance degrades substantially under realistic wearable sensor conditions.

label: Collaborative HAR with Passive Inter-Body Electrostatic Field
authors: Sizhen Bian, Vitor Fortes Rey, Siyu Yuan, Paul Lukowicz
headline: Evaluates novel electrostatic field sensing for HAR. Negative result: less competitive than accelerometer alone.
scan_relevance: Negative result demonstrating novel sensing modality underperforms vs standard accelerometer for HAR.

label: Validation of Inertial Sensor-Based Step Detection Algorithms for Edge Device De
authors: Kisiel M, Amjad A, Szczęsna A
headline: Validates and compares five fundamentally different step detection algorithms using IMU data, with a focus on deployment to edge devices.
